subject: Re: Can't run, is this a known thing?
body:
Hi Bart,

I'm afraid there aren't many known things yet as this is the first help 
request since release :-) 

Usually, when GPUAcceleration.dll "can't be found", it's because of a 
missing dependency. The only immediate dependency not supplied with the 
installer is vcomp140.dll – that's the VC++ redistributable 
<https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?LinkId=746572>. Please make sure to 
install it.

Another issue might be an outdated GPU driver 
<http://www.nvidia.com/Download/index.aspx?lang=en-us>, or a GPU 
architecture older than Maxwell. If that isn't the case, could you please 
describe your system configuration, and what you've installed so far?

Cheers,
Dimitry



On Tuesday, June 26, 2018 at 3:39:56 PM UTC+2, Bart Alewijnse wrote:
>
> Hi, I can't start wrap. 
>
> It gives me a dialog box complaining it can't find CUDA devices (which I 
> think isn't correct, e.g. CUDA-Z seems happy, and because...).
>
> ...At first with the explanation that it couldn't find GPUAcceleration.dll 
> (in the same directory)
>
>
> I then tried to compile the thing from source, which I failed to do 
> (dependency blah, gave up),
> but this seems to have changed the dialog box's explanation to a 
> System.Exception in Warp.MainWindow..ctor()
>
>
> This sounds like it may be a simple stupid thing I'm doing wrong?
>
>
